title: Manufacturing Journey Assessment - Set 2
description: Assessment covering the stainless steel manufacturing process from Raw Material Procurement to Dispatch.

questions.question: Which raw material provides chromium in stainless steel production?
questions.explanation: Ferro Chromium provides chromium, which gives stainless steel its corrosion resistance.

questions.question: Which area receives, inspects and segregates scrap before melting?
questions.explanation: The Scrap Yard prepares scrap before charging it into the EAF.

questions.question: Which component produces the electric arc inside an Electric Arc Furnace?
questions.explanation: Graphite electrodes generate the electric arc required for melting.

questions.question: What gas is mainly mixed with oxygen during the AOD process?
questions.explanation: Argon helps reduce chromium oxidation during decarburization.

questions.question: Which process immediately follows the AOD converter?
questions.explanation: The molten steel is transferred to the Ladle Refining Furnace.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the tundish in Continuous Casting?
options: Distribute molten steel to the mould
questions.correctAnswer: Distribute molten steel to the mould
questions.explanation: The tundish acts as an intermediate reservoir before casting.

questions.question: What is the first operation in Hot Rolling?
questions.explanation: Slabs are reheated before entering the rolling mill.

questions.question: Cold Rolling is normally carried out at:
questions.explanation: Cold Rolling is performed without reheating the material.

questions.question: Which process restores ductility after Cold Rolling?
questions.explanation: Annealing relieves internal stresses and restores ductility.

questions.question: Which process improves corrosion resistance by removing oxide scale?
questions.explanation: Pickling removes oxide scales formed during heat treatment.

questions.question: Molten steel is transferred from EAF directly to CCM.
questions.explanation: It passes through AOD and LRF before Continuous Casting.

questions.question: Warehouse operations include inventory management.
questions.explanation: Inventory management is one of the warehouse's primary responsibilities.

questions.question: Quality inspection takes place throughout manufacturing.
questions.explanation: Quality checks are performed at every important stage.

questions.question: The LRF is used for secondary refining of molten steel.
questions.explanation: The Ladle Refining Furnace performs secondary refining and temperature adjustment.

questions.question: Dispatch takes place before Quality Inspection.
questions.explanation: Finished products are inspected before they are dispatched.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the Scrap Yard?
questions.sampleAnswer: The Scrap Yard receives, inspects, segregates and prepares scrap for charging into the Electric Arc Furnace.

questions.question: Why is Argon used in the AOD process?
questions.sampleAnswer: Argon reduces chromium oxidation while helping remove carbon during refining.

questions.question: What is the function of the Ladle Refining Furnace (LRF)?
questions.sampleAnswer: The LRF adjusts temperature, refines molten steel and corrects chemical composition.

questions.question: Why is Pickling performed after Annealing?
questions.sampleAnswer: Pickling removes oxide scale formed during annealing and restores a clean corrosion-resistant surface.

questions.question: Mention any two responsibilities of the Warehouse department.
questions.sampleAnswer: Warehouse stores materials safely, manages inventory, issues materials and prepares finished goods for dispatch.

questions.question: Which equipment continuously withdraws the slab during Continuous Casting?
questions.explanation: Withdrawal rollers continuously pull the solidifying slab from the mould.

questions.question: Which process is primarily responsible for improving surface finish and dimensional accuracy?
questions.explanation: Cold Rolling produces accurate thickness and an improved surface finish.

questions.question: Which inspection is commonly performed after Grinding and Finishing?
questions.explanation: Grinding is followed by surface quality inspection.

questions.question: Which document confirms that the finished product meets required specifications?
questions.explanation: The Quality Certificate confirms compliance with quality requirements.

questions.question: Which sequence correctly represents the steelmaking stages before rolling?
questions.explanation: Molten steel is melted in the EAF, refined in the AOD and LRF, then cast in the CCM.
